phase.name: DatabaseAdapter Infrastructure Hardening
phase.admission_condition: Phase 0 evidence inventory (existing artifacts: migration_plan_pg_duckdb.v2.json, database_migration_caller_inventory.v1.json, database_migration_schema_column_audit.v1.json, database_migration_effect_drizzle_audit.v1.json) is complete

context_summary: The DatabaseAdapter seam exists at storage/adapter.ts but (1) is concretely typed to SQLite (DrizzleClient = SQLite union), (2) assumes sync callbacks (Postgres is async), (3) has no afterCommit method (needed by sync/index.ts process()), (4) has no retry support for Postgres deadlocks, (5) has no tests, (6) has transaction options locked to SQLite behavior enum. The effect-drizzle-sqlite package provides Effect-yieldable SQLite but 16/19 files are SQLite-only. The existing plan (migration_plan_pg_duckdb.v2.json Phase 1) defines the DatabaseAdapter conversion but the code has drifted from the plan.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: DatabaseAdapter.Interface.DrizzleClient type is concretely inferred from SQLite's Database.use callback parameter (TxOrDb = SQLiteTransaction | SQLiteBunDatabase). A PgAdapter cannot be type-safe against this interface. The interface must be generic over client type.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: Make DrizzleClient a type parameter on the Interface (or a union with dialect tag). The callbacks must accept both SQLite and Postgres client types.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: Interface.query<T>(fn) assumes sync callback (fn returns T, not Promise<T>). Postgres Drizzle operations are async. This breaks for any async consumer.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: The interface must support both sync and async callbacks. fn should return T | Promise<T> or the interface should use Effect's native type system.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: No afterCommit method in Interface. sync/index.ts process() calls Database.effect() for post-commit bus publishing. Without afterCommit, PgAdapter cannot support the transactional outbox pattern.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: Add afterCommit(fn: () => void | Promise<void>): Effect.Effect<void> to Interface. Implement in SQLiteAdapter via LocalContext. Refactor sync/index.ts to use adapter.afterCommit.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: No retry support for Postgres deadlocks. All adapter.transaction call sites in sync/index.ts use Effect.catch(Effect.die). Postgres serialization failures (40001) and deadlocks (40P01) will kill fibers instead of retrying.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: Add discriminated DatabaseError (RetriableDatabaseError | FatalDatabaseError). Add retry schedule to adapter.transaction with configurable attempts.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: Transaction options { behavior: 'deferred' | 'immediate' | 'exclusive' } are SQLite-specific. Postgres uses isolation levels and access modes. The plan specifies { isolation?: string } but the code has { behavior }.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: Define discriminated transaction options union: { tag: 'sqlite'; behavior: ... } | { tag: 'postgres'; isolationLevel: ... } or use a common base type.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: DatabaseAdapter has zero tests despite being a critical production seam used by 21+ source modules. No test infrastructure for multi-backend testing. Typecheck status is unverifiable.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: Create adapter.unit.test.ts, establish multi-backend test patterns, verify typecheck passes.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: Timestamps helper uses integer() with $default(() => Date.now()) — SQLite-specific. No timestampsPg() helper exists for Postgres timestamp() types. $onUpdate has no Postgres DDL equivalent.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: Create timestampsPg() helper using pg-core timestamp() with default(sql`now()`). Document $onUpdate gap and app-layer replacement.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: defaultLayer is hardcoded to SQLiteAdapter. 5 runtime wiring files independently reference DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer. No switching mechanism for multiple backends.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: After interface hardening, add a backend switching mechanism (feature flag or layer factory). Do not change runtime wiring until the switching mechanism exists.

blocking_defects_from_critique.description: 14 raw Database.use/transaction/effect calls remain in 7 files. 2 files have TODO(gov-migration) comments. Three files (data-migration.ts, projectors.ts, fence.ts) have zero adapter adoption.
blocking_defects_from_critique.repair: Convert remaining raw calls to use DatabaseAdapter.Service. Address TODO(gov-migration) blockers in projectors.ts and fence.ts. NOTE: deferred to a follow-up phase if the context boundary requires it (projectors.ts and fence.ts depend on sync APIs that don't support Effect).

acceptance_criteria: DatabaseAdapter.Interface accepts both sync (SQLite) and async (Postgres) callbacks without type errors
DatabaseAdapter.Interface has afterCommit method; SQLiteAdapter implements it; sync/index.ts process() uses adapter.afterCommit instead of Database.effect()
DatabaseAdapter.Interface has discriminated error types (RetriableDatabaseError | FatalDatabaseError)
SQLiteAdapter unit tests exist and pass
@effect/sql-pg dependency added to effect-drizzle-sqlite and pg.test.ts mirrors sqlite.test.ts
timestampsPg() helper exists with pg-core timestamp() types
Typecheck passes with zero errors (baseline established)
Existing database_migration_plan_pg_duckdb.v2.json remains valid as the long-term roadmap
deferred_seams: Raw Database.* calls in data-migration.ts, projectors.ts, fence.ts — deferred to Phase 1b because projectors and fence depend on sync-only APIs that cannot consume Effect yet
Complete PgAdapter implementation — deferred to Phase 2 (schema conversion phase)
pgTable schema definitions — deferred to Phase 2
Runtime backend switching (usePostgres flag) — deferred to Phase 3
effect-drizzle-pg package creation — deferred to Phase 2 (may not need a full package if PGlite works with straight drizzle-orm/pg-core)

execution_steps.description: Fix DatabaseAdapter.Interface type to be backend-agnostic
execution_steps.scope: Replace concretely-typed DrizzleClient with a generic type parameter or union. Support both sync and async callbacks. Define backend-agnostic transaction options. Add afterCommit to Interface.

execution_steps.description: Implement SQLiteAdapter updates matching new Interface
execution_steps.scope: SQLiteAdapter.query, SQLiteAdapter.transaction, SQLiteAdapter.afterCommit all conform to new Interface. Use LocalContext for afterCommit.

execution_steps.description: Add discriminated DatabaseError types with retry support
execution_steps.scope: Define RetriableDatabaseError (pgcode, isRetryable: true) and FatalDatabaseError. Add retry schedule to adapter.transaction.
Test query, transaction, afterCommit, error wrapping, retry behavior. Use in-memory SQLite via effect-drizzle-sqlite pattern.
Replace Database.effect() with adapter.afterCommit in process(). Ensure the post-commit bus publishing works through the adapter interface.
execution_steps.test_command: bun test packages/opencode/test/sync/ or bun test --filter sync

execution_steps.scope: Add @effect/sql-pg to devDependencies. Create pg.test.ts mirroring sqlite.test.ts patterns but using pgTable, PgClient.layer(), and pg-core Drizzle types.
Add timestampsPg() using pg-core's timestamp() or timestamptz() with default(sql`now()`). Keep existing Timestamps (sqlite) unchanged. Export both.
Run bun typecheck in packages/opencode. Fix any pre-existing errors. Record the zero-error state.

execution_notes: Steps 1.1-1.3 can be done in a single commit since they all modify adapter.ts. Steps 1.4 and 1.5 depend on 1.1-1.3. Steps 1.6 and 1.7 are independent and can be parallelized with 1.4-1.5. Step 1.8 is a validation step at the end.
